data.title: Nvidia in spotlight as Morgan Stanley 'more positive' after GTC analyst Q&A
data.description:
Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) was in the spotlight again on Thursday as Morgan Stanley said it was “more positive” on the tech giant's trajectory after it held an analyst question and answer session at its GTC event.

Shares fell fractionally in premarket trading.

Morgan Stanley analyst Joseph Moore said management's enthusiasm at this stage of the Blackwell was “notable,” given that it comes at a time when the market appears to be losing confidence. Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang said during the keynote address that the four major cloud service providers — Amazon Web Services, Azure, Google Cloud Platform and Oracle Cloud — have already purchased 3.6M Blackwell GPUs in 2025, compared to 1.3M Hopper GPUs in 2024. 

“During the Q&A session it was clear that the reason the company made the decision to give that data was to refocus the narrative on the strength of the demand profile, as they continue to field questions related to Open AI related spending shifting from 1 of the 4 to another of the 4, or the pressure of ASICs, which come from these 4 customers,” Moore wrote in a note to clients.

“The company sees [cloud service provider] capex as solid through 2025, and while they have not projected 2026 revenue prospects — we wouldn’t expect them to — they did say that they expect cloud and IT spending to grow for several years and for AI to take a larger portion of that spending, which certainly would imply that they expect growth. The 3.6M Blackwell number also reflects orders for Blackwell so far from those customers, with demand 'obviously much greater.' Every cloud business has gone through periods of digestion over time, and NVIDIA is not going to be immune from that, but to us all indicators continue to point to strong growth for several quarters.”

Moore reiterated his Overweight rating and $162 price target on Nvidia.

Nvidia management also made a “compelling case” on how to solve the problem of growing inferencing at scale. And while the market is skittish over efficiencies, it should drive demand higher, especially as application specific integrated circuits start to encroach on Nvidia's business, Moore added.

“To depend on an ASIC development to serve future demand given the diversity of workloads and the large capital commitments. With a million GPU clusters set to emerge in the coming years, relying on an ASIC roadmap to get to where Nvidia has already told you they would be, seems risky.”

data.title: Revival Gold enters marketing services agreement
data.description:
• Revival Gold (OTCQX:RVLGF) has entered into a marketing service agreement with Resource Stock Digest of Round Rock, Texas.
  • Under the terms, among other things, RSD has agreed to provide certain promotional services to the Co. in accordance with Policy 3.4 – Investor Relations, Promotional and Market-Making Activities of the TSX Venture Exchange.
  • RSD has been engaged for a 3-month advertising and marketing program for a cash fee of $0.1M payable upon receipt of approval of the Agreement by the Exchange.
  • As at the date of the Agreement, the aggregate holdings of Mr. Del Real and Mr. Hodge represent less than 1% of the issued shares of Revival Gold.

data.title: Canadian Solar signs battery supply deal for Strata Clean Energy storage project in Arizona
data.description:
Canadian Solar (NASDAQ:CSIQ) said Thursday it signed a battery supply agreement with Strata Clean Energy for a 576 MWh DC battery energy storage system in Arizona; financial terms were not disclosed.

Canadian Solar (NASDAQ:CSIQ) said Strata's White Tank Battery project will deliver stored renewable energy to Arizona Public Service utilizing its SolBank 3.0 BESS technology to supply, commission and oversee the long-term operation of the battery system.

The company will provide ~120 SolBank 3.0 battery enclosures to the White Tank Battery project to help shift renewable energy produced in the middle of the day to the early evening hours; following commissioning, it will manage the system under a 20-year service agreement.

Construction is expected to begin in October 2026.

data.title: UnitedHealth’s Optum Rx overhauls pharmacy payment model
data.description:
UnitedHealth’s (NYSE:UNH) pharmacy benefit manager, Optum Rx, announced Thursday the launch of cost-based payment models to support its non-affiliated network pharmacies.

The company said cost-based reimbursements are expected to address a main issue with a legacy, industry-wide payment model, which it said had initially been designed to support generic drug adoption but raised costs for pharmacies as more and more expensive generics entered the market.

“Addressing this imbalance will provide pharmacies with better financial means to stock more medicines, which will alleviate drug shortages, improve access to medications, and deliver a better experience for consumers,” Optum said.

The company added that it has already started to roll out the changes, with a full implementation of the revised reimbursement model expected by January 2028.

Major drug retailers, CVS Health (CVS) and Walgreens Boots Alliance (WBA) have flagged declining reimbursement as a major concern impacting their pharmacy operations in recent years.

“This move by Optum Rx is a significant step forward in changing the reimbursement model for our pharmacies, who continue to struggle with the rising costs of medications,” said Bretta Grinsteinner, an executive responsible for dealing with PBMs on behalf of Epic Pharmacy Network.

data.title: EU countermeasures to 'unjustified' Trump tariffs to take effect mid-April
data.description:
EU countermeasures to U.S. tariffs announced on March 12, are likely to take effect in mid-April, European Trade Commissioner Maros Sefcovic said on Thursday. 

"In the light of the recent announcement that the U.S. is planning to introduce additional tariffs on 2 April, we are now considering to align the timing of the two sets of EU countermeasures, so we can consult with Member States on both lists simultaneously," Sefcovic said in a statement.

The Commission has also launched a new process for the adoption of further measures to respond to the expansion of the U.S. section 232 tariffs hitting €18 billion of EU exports, Sefcovic further said, adding, he has been engaging with his U.S. counterparts to better understand the plans and look for possible solutions.

Earlier this month, the EU said it would raise its levies on up to €26 billion worth of U.S. goods, including boats, bourbon and motorbikes, from 1 April.

Calling the Trump administration's tariffs "unjustified", Sefcovic said the EU is "clearly not the source of the problem when it comes to steel and aluminium. The real problem is overcapacity."

The European Union said Wednesday it proposes to reduce steel imports by a further 15% starting in April, in a move aimed at preventing cheap steel flooding the European market after the U.S. imposed tariffs.

As per European Council data, the EU had a trade surplus of €156.6 billion with the U.S. for goods in 2023. For services, the bloc had a trade deficit of €108.6 billion. Overall EU-U.S. trade in goods and services in 2023 was €1.6 trillion.

Here are some exchange-traded funds that track the benchmark S&P 500 index (SP500): (SPY), (VOO), (IVV), (RSP), (SSO), (UPRO), (SH), (SDS), and (SPXU).

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWG), (GF), (NYSEARCA:EWI), (EWQ), (FGM), (DAX), (FLGR), (FXB), (EWU), (FKU), (BATS:EWUS), (NYSEARCA:FLGB), (GREK)

data.title: Darden Restaurants dips after same-restaurant sales at Olive Garden, LongHorn Steakhouse disappoint
data.description:
Darden Restaurants (NYSE:DRI) reported sales increased by 6.4% in FQ3 to $3.16 billion, driven by a blended same-restaurant sales increase of 0.7% vs. 1.8% consensus and extra sales from the addition of 103 Chuy's restaurants and 40 net new restaurants.

Same-restaurant sales were up 0.6% for the Olive Garden chain for the quarter that ended on February 23 and fell 0.8% for the company's fine dining chains, which include Ruth's Chris and Capital Grille. The LongHorn Steakhouse chain saw a 2.6% increase in same-restaurant sales during the quarter to miss the consensus expectation of +5.0%.

Segment profit was up at Olive Garden compared to a year ago ($306.6 million vs. $294.7 million) and LongHorn Steakhouse ($149.3 million vs. $136.6 million). EPS came in at $2.80 vs. $2.79 consensus and $2.67 a year ago.

"We had a solid quarter, and I am proud of how our teams managed their business and controlled what they could contro" stated Darden President & CEO Rick Cardenas. "All of our segments grew total sales and segment profit margin, while several brands set sales records during the holidays and on Valentine's Day, reinforcing the strength of our portfolio and the loyalty of our guests. Our ability to deliver profitable sales growth in a challenging environment is a testament to the strength of our business model and adherence to our proven strategy," he added.

During FQ3, the restaurant company repurchased approximately 0.3 million shares of its stock for a total of $53 million. As of the end of FQ3, the company had $548 million remaining under the current $1 billion repurchase authorization.

In terms of guidance, Darden Restaurants (NYSE:DRI) sees full-year EPS of $9.45 to $9.52 (midpoint $9.485) vs. $9.49 consensus. Full-year comparable sales growth of +1.5% is anticipated, vs. +1.3% consensus. The company expects to open 50 to 55 new restaurants during the year.

Shares of Darden Restaurants (DRI) slipped 0.1% in premarket action on Thursday to $188.01 vs. the 52-week trading range of $135.87 to $203.12.

data.title: Accenture beats Q2 estimates as Managed Services drive revenue growth
data.description:
Shares of Accenture (NYSE:ACN) rose about 1% premarket on Thursday after fiscal second quarter results beat estimates and the company slightly narrowed its revenue growth outlook for fiscal 2025.

For the fiscal second quarter ended Feb. 28, revenue rose 5% in U.S. dollar terms (8.5% in local currency) year-over-year to $16.7B, while GAAP EPS climbed 7% year-on-year to $2.82. Both top and bottom line numbers beat estimates.

"The trust and confidence in our unique strengths and capabilities is reflected in 32 clients with quarterly bookings greater than $100 million and we are very pleased to have another milestone quarter in Gen AI with $1.4 billion in new bookings," said Accenture Chair and CEO Julie Sweet.

The Irish company's Consulting revenues in the second quarter grew 3% (in U.S. dollars) year-over-year to $8.28B, while Managed Services revenues jumped 8% (in U.S. dollars) to $8.38B.

Americas was the top geographic market by revenue, generating $8.55B in the fiscal second quarter, growing 9% (in U.S. dollars) year-over-year.

Generative AI new bookings for the fiscal second quarter were $1.4B.

However, the company noted that new bookings for the second quarter of fiscal 2025 were $20.91B, declining 3% in U.S. dollars and flat in local currency, compared to the second quarter of fiscal 2024.

Dividend: Accenture declared a $1.48 quarterly dividend, the same as the previous quarter's amount. The record date is April 10, and the dividend is payable on May 15.

Stock Buyback: During the second quarter, Accenture bought back or redeemed 4M shares, including about 2.4M shares repurchased in the open market, for a total of $1.4B. Accenture's total remaining share repurchase authority, at Feb. 28, was around $5B.

Outlook: Accenture expects third quarter revenue to be between $16.9B and $17.5B (midpoint at $17.2B), compared to consensus estimate of $17.22B.

Fiscal year 2025: The company narrowed its revenue growth to be in the range of 5% to 7% (midpoint at 6%) in local currency, compared to 4% to 7% previously, consensus revenue growth is 5.73%.

The company continues to expect foreign-exchange impact of about negative 0.5% on results.

Related stocks: Cognizant Technology Solutions (CTSH), EPAM Systems (EPAM) and Infosys (INFY) were largely flat premarket on Thursday.

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: Accenture tops FQ2 estimates; initiates FQ3 and narrows FY rev growth
The humanoid robot revolution is upon us - Nvidia CEO
data.description:
A new "iPhone moment" is being hailed by the robotics industry, with a powerful era underway for manufacturing and domestic assistance. "When, literally, humanoid robots are wandering around, is not five years away, this is a few years away," Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) CEO Jensen Huang said this week at the GTC conference. The company, along with others, already has a lot of skin in the game, with its RTX GPUs powering bots and the firm's coming Isaac GR00T N1 serving as a generalist foundation model.

Next generation: As the staple of any sci-fi movie in the past, humanoid robots are now moving closer to reality due to advances in artificial general intelligence. It's part of what's being called "embodied AI," which harnesses AI technology inside of hardware - in this case a robot that can carry out real-world functions. Up until recently, most bots relied on code to perform their tasks, which limited their applications. The promise of AI will allow them to learn new jobs by reasoning and imitation, or eventually identifying and solving situations like a human by "generalizing across common tasks."

"We know very clearly that the world has severe shortages of human laborers, human workers," Huang continued. Humanoid robots never need to take a day off, can work around the clock, and are able to be used in multiple settings. That includes the workforce, like in factory environments or the assembly line, as well as the home, for tasks such as vacuuming, folding laundry, doing dishes, and even companionship.

In the race: One of the biggest known players is Tesla's (NASDAQ:TSLA) Optimus, which Elon Musk has called the "biggest product in history" with plans to produce up to 1M units by 2027 (could it help reignite the stock?). Another U.S.-based player is Brett Adcock's Figure AI, which is backed by Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT) and OpenAI. The company's bots are geared toward manual labor tasks like logistics and warehousing, and have already been deployed in BMW factories. Others out there include Atlas by Boston Dynamics and Phoenix by Sanctuary AI, as well as Chinese bots like Unitree's G1, CyberOne by Xiaomi and UBTECH's Walker S1, so be on the lookout for more industry partnerships and innovation.
